title: JEPAMatch: Geometric Representation Shaping for Semi-Supervised Learning
authors: Ali Aghababaei-Harandi; Aude Sportisse; Massih-Reza Amini
principal_argument: Semi-supervised learning can be improved by explicitly shaping latent geometry, not only by thresholded pseudo-labeling, using LeJEPA-inspired representation regularization.
methodology: Adds a latent-space regularization term derived from LeJEPA to the classical FlexMatch semi-supervised objective and evaluates the combined method on CIFAR-100, STL-10, and Tiny-ImageNet.
main_findings: The method reportedly outperforms existing baselines across the tested datasets, converges faster, and reduces computational cost relative to standard FixMatch-style pipelines.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract emphasizes limitations of prior FixMatch-style methods but does not specify concrete limitations of the proposed method.
future_opportunities: Not explicitly stated in the abstract.

title: Infrastructure-Centric World Models: Bridging Temporal Depth and Spatial Breadth for Roadside Perception
authors: Siyuan Meng; Chengbo Ai
principal_argument: Infrastructure-centric world models offer a complementary alternative to ego-vehicle world models because fixed roadside systems provide persistent, multi-sensor temporal coverage.
methodology: Presents a three-phase roadmap for infrastructure-centric world models, a dual-layer architecture, a phased sensor strategy, a taxonomy of world-model paradigms, and the proposed Infrastructure VLA concept.
main_findings: The paper positions infrastructure-centric modeling as a distinct opportunity, identifies open-source building blocks for each phase, and frames latent-space alignment for V2X collaboration as a key direction.
limitations_or_gaps: This is a vision and taxonomy paper; the abstract does not report a validated end-to-end empirical system.
future_opportunities: Develop the three proposed phases, collaborative V2X latent-space alignment, and the Infrastructure VLA framework.

title: Revisiting Auxiliary Losses for Conditional Depth Routing: An Empirical Study
authors: Qingwei Lin
principal_argument: Common auxiliary losses for conditional depth routing can be harmful under the studied setup, and the benefit of a JEPA-guided gate depends strongly on the supervision recipe.
methodology: Runs a controlled empirical comparison between an MLP gate and a JEPA-guided gate in a 157.5M-parameter decoder-only model with controller-only training, 50% full-path budget, three seeds, and ablations on auxiliary losses.
main_findings: With util/rank supervision, the JEPA-guided gate improves early-to-mid optimization, but removing util/rank improves language-model loss and threshold-hit speed for both gates and cuts the training FLOPs proxy substantially.
limitations_or_gaps: The authors explicitly state that the conclusions are scoped to the studied regime.
future_opportunities: The findings motivate alternative supervision strategies that better match gated execution instead of off-policy oracle labels.

title: The Global Neural World Model: Spatially Grounded Discrete Topologies for Action-Conditioned Planning
authors: Noureddine Kermiche
principal_argument: A continuous, action-conditioned JEPA with discrete spatial topology can support stable rollouts and structured planning without pixel-level reconstruction.
methodology: Introduces the Global Neural World Model, which maps environments to a discrete 2D grid using entropy-based topological quantization, translational equivariance, grid snapping, and maximum-entropy exploration via random walks.
main_findings: The abstract claims the model prevents manifold drift during autoregressive rollouts and organizes continuous concepts into structured topological maps across passive, active, and abstract sequence settings.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract does not describe specific failure cases or detailed benchmark comparisons beyond broad validation claims.
future_opportunities: Not explicitly stated in the abstract.

title: Stylistic-STORM (ST-STORM) : Perceiving the Semantic Nature of Appearance
authors: Hamed Ouattara; Pierre Duthon; Pascal Houssam Salmane; Frédéric Bernardin; Omar Ait Aider
principal_argument: Appearance should be treated as semantic signal rather than nuisance when tasks depend on style cues such as weather effects or clinical appearance.
methodology: Proposes ST-STORM, a hybrid self-supervised framework with separate content and style latent streams, using a JEPA-plus-contrastive content branch and a prediction/reconstruction-based style branch under adversarial constraint.
main_findings: The style branch reportedly isolates complex appearance phenomena with strong results on weather and ISIC tasks while preserving content-branch semantic performance on ImageNet-1K.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract reports headline metrics but does not spell out specific limitations of the approach.
future_opportunities: Not explicitly stated in the abstract.

title: From Alignment to Prediction: A Study of Self-Supervised Learning and Predictive Representation Learning
authors: Mintu Dutta; Ritesh Vyas; Mohendra Roy
principal_argument: Predictive Representation Learning is a distinct self-supervised paradigm centered on latent prediction of unobserved data, with JEPA as a representative example.
methodology: Defines a taxonomy spanning alignment, reconstruction, and predictive representation learning, and implements BYOL, MAE, and I-JEPA for comparative analysis.
main_findings: The paper argues for PRL as a promising direction and reports that MAE achieves perfect similarity but weaker robustness, while BYOL and I-JEPA trade slightly lower reported accuracy for stronger robustness.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract highlights theoretical questions and open challenges in predictive representation learning rather than detailing specific study limitations.
future_opportunities: Further work on the theoretical perspectives and open challenges of predictive representation learning.

title: Grounded World Model for Semantically Generalizable Planning
authors: Quanyi Li; Lan Feng; Haonan Zhang; Wuyang Li; Letian Wang; Alexandre Alahi; Harold Soh
principal_argument: World models grounded in a vision-language-aligned latent space can let model-predictive control use natural-language task instructions instead of goal images and improve semantic generalization.
methodology: Learns a Grounded World Model whose predicted future outcomes are scored by embedding similarity to a task instruction, and evaluates the resulting GWM-MPC system on the WISER benchmark.
main_findings: GWM-MPC achieves an 87% success rate on 288 test tasks with unseen visual signals and referring expressions, versus an average 22% success rate for traditional VLAs.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract motivates the method using shortcomings of goal-image conditioning but does not identify concrete limitations of the proposed model itself.
future_opportunities: Not explicitly stated in the abstract.

title: The Missing Knowledge Layer in Cognitive Architectures for AI Agents
authors: Michaël Roynard
principal_argument: AI cognitive architectures need an explicit knowledge layer with its own persistence semantics; otherwise factual knowledge and episodic memory are handled incorrectly.
methodology: Surveys persistence semantics across memory systems, identifies convergence points across prior work, proposes a four-layer decomposition, and includes companion implementations in Python and Rust.
main_findings: The paper argues that current frameworks such as CoALA and JEPA lack this separation and that distinct persistence semantics are necessary for engineering implementations.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract is primarily architectural and argumentative; it does not report benchmark-style empirical validation beyond feasibility demonstrations.
future_opportunities: Design agent systems that implement separate persistence semantics for knowledge, memory, wisdom, and intelligence.

title: GeoMeld: Toward Semantically Grounded Foundation Models for Remote Sensing
authors: Maram Hasan; Md Aminur Hossain; Savitra Roy; Souparna Bhowmik; Ayush V. Patel; Mainak Singha; Subhasis Chaudhuri; Muhammad Haris Khan; Biplab Banerjee
principal_argument: Remote-sensing foundation models need large-scale aligned multimodal data and grounded language supervision, which GeoMeld and GeoMeld-FM are intended to provide.
methodology: Builds a roughly 2.5 million-sample multimodal dataset with agentic captioning and introduces a pretraining framework combining masked autoencoding, JEPA representation learning, and caption-vision contrastive alignment.
main_findings: The abstract reports consistent gains in downstream transfer and cross-sensor robustness and presents the dataset-framework pair as a scalable reference for the field.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract emphasizes limitations of prior remote-sensing resources but does not specify concrete limitations of GeoMeld or GeoMeld-FM.
future_opportunities: Extend semantically grounded multimodal foundation modeling in remote sensing using this scalable reference framework.

title: Data-Efficient Surgical Phase Segmentation in Small-Incision Cataract Surgery: A Controlled Study of Vision Foundation Models
authors: Lincoln Spencer; Song Wang; Chen Chen
principal_argument: Large self-supervised vision foundation models can improve low-label surgical phase segmentation when evaluated under a controlled temporal-model setup.
methodology: Compares supervised encoders and foundation models including DINOv3 and V-JEPA2 by pairing each with the same MS-TCN++ temporal model on SICS-155 and using a cached-feature pipeline.
main_findings: Foundation-model features improve segmentation performance, with DINOv3 ViT-7B achieving the best reported results at 83.4% accuracy and an 87.0 edit score.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract notes that cataract-domain transfer and lightweight adaptation can help or hurt, but it does not provide detailed failure analysis.
future_opportunities: Not explicitly stated in the abstract.

title: Efficient Hierarchical Implicit Flow Q-learning for Offline Goal-conditioned Reinforcement Learning
authors: Zhiqiang Dong; Teng Pang; Rongjian Xu; Guoqiang Wu
principal_argument: More expressive hierarchical policies and more discriminative goal representations can improve offline goal-conditioned reinforcement learning on long-horizon tasks.
methodology: Introduces a goal-conditioned mean flow policy for high- and low-level policies and adds a LeJEPA loss that repels goal embeddings during training; evaluates on state-based and pixel-based OGBench tasks.
main_findings: The method is reported to achieve strong performance across both state-based and pixel-based benchmarks.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract describes shortcomings of prior methods but does not specify concrete limitations of the proposed approach.
future_opportunities: Not explicitly stated in the abstract.

title: Multimodal Latent Reasoning via Predictive Embeddings
authors: Ashutosh Adhikari; Mirella Lapata
principal_argument: Predictive embedding learning in latent space can replace explicit tool calls at inference time for multimodal reasoning while preserving standard VLM generation pipelines.
methodology: Proposes Pearl, a JEPA-inspired method that learns from expert tool-use trajectories entirely in latent space, and compares it with supervised fine-tuning and reconstruction-based latent reasoning on perception benchmarks.
main_findings: Pearl reportedly matches or outperforms the comparison methods, and the study argues that reconstruction-based latent reasoning mainly learns embeddings rather than image edits.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract critiques prior tool-augmented and reconstruction-based approaches but does not identify explicit limitations of Pearl itself.
future_opportunities: The results motivate predictive embedding learning as an alternative to reconstruction-based latent reasoning for multimodal systems.

title: Beyond the Beep: Scalable Collision Anticipation and Real-Time Explainability with BADAS-2.0
authors: Roni Goldshmidt; Hamish Scott; Lorenzo Niccolini; Hernan Matzner
principal_argument: Scaling data curation, domain-specific self-supervised pretraining, distillation, and explainability together can materially improve collision anticipation systems.
methodology: Expands labeled data using BADAS-1.0 as an active oracle plus targeted collection, pretrains on 2.25 million unlabeled driving videos, distills to compact edge models, and adds attention heatmaps and a vision-language reasoning module.
main_findings: BADAS-2.0 reports consistent gains across long-tail subgroups, the largest improvements on the hardest cases, 7-12x speedups with near-parity accuracy for distilled edge models, and real-time explanatory outputs.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract focuses on reported gains and released assets rather than discussing explicit limitations of the system.
future_opportunities: Public benchmarks, inference code, and the BADAS-Reason setup create opportunities for further work on explainable driver-assistance systems.

title: PI-JEPA: Label-Free Surrogate Pretraining for Coupled Multiphysics Simulation via Operator-Split Latent Prediction
authors: Brandon Yee; Pairie Koh
principal_argument: Label-free pretraining on unlabeled reservoir parameter fields can substantially reduce the labeled simulation budget for multiphysics neural surrogates.
methodology: PI-JEPA uses masked latent prediction on unlabeled parameter fields with per-sub-operator PDE residual regularization, with a predictor bank aligned to Lie-Trotter operator splitting and separate latent modules for pressure, saturation transport, and reaction before fine-tuning on limited labeled runs.
main_findings: On single-phase Darcy flow, PI-JEPA reports 1.9x lower error than FNO and 2.4x lower error than DeepONet at 100 labeled runs, plus a 24% gain over supervised-only training at 500 labeled runs.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract-level evidence is centered on single-phase Darcy flow and low-label fine-tuning settings, so broader multiphysics generalization is not established here.
future_opportunities: Test the pretraining scheme on broader multiphysics operators and more realistic reservoir simulation settings.

title: Deep Networks Favor Simple Data
authors: Weyl Lu; Chenjie Hao; Yubei Chen
principal_argument: The classic OOD density anomaly reflects a broader pattern: deep networks across architectures and density estimators systematically assign higher density to simpler data.
methodology: Separates the trained network from the density estimator, introduces Jacobian-based and autoregressive self-estimators, and studies multiple model families including I-JEPA with external complexity metrics.
main_findings: The study finds highly consistent density rankings favoring simpler samples within datasets and across OOD pairs, with strong agreement across independently trained models and with complexity metrics.
limitations_or_gaps: The authors explicitly state that the goal is to define and visualize the question more clearly, not to settle it completely.
future_opportunities: Further investigate why deep networks favor simple data across architectures, objectives, and density estimators.

title: Mine-JEPA: In-Domain Self-Supervised Learning for Mine-Like Object Classification in Side-Scan Sonar
authors: Taeyoun Kwon; Youngwon Choi; Hyeonyu Kim; Myeongkyun Cho; Junhyeok Choi; Moon Hwan Kim
principal_argument: Carefully designed in-domain self-supervised learning can outperform far larger general-purpose vision foundation models for side-scan sonar mine classification under data scarcity.
methodology: Mine-JEPA pretrains with the SIGReg self-supervised loss on 1,170 unlabeled sonar images, then evaluates binary and 3-class mine-like object classification, including a compact ViT-Tiny setting and synthetic data augmentation.
main_findings: The method reaches F1 0.935 for mine vs. non-mine and 0.820 for 3-class classification with augmentation, outperforming fine-tuned DINOv3 in both settings; applying in-domain SSL to foundation models degrades performance by 10 to 13 points, and the compact backbone remains competitive with 4x fewer parameters.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary is limited to side-scan sonar classification with a small unlabeled corpus and binary/3-class tasks.
future_opportunities: Evaluate the approach on broader sonar datasets and clarify when domain adaptation helps or harms stronger pretrained models.

title: Scaling Video Pretraining for Surgical Foundation Models
authors: Sicheng Lu; Zikai Xiao; Jianhui Wei; Danyu Sun; Qi Lu; Keli Hu; Yang Feng; Jian Wu; Zongxin Yang; Zuozhu Liu
principal_argument: Surgical video understanding needs a scalable and reproducible pretraining recipe and benchmark, and JEPA-based pretraining is a strong candidate foundation.
methodology: Curates a 10,535-video, 214.5 million-frame corpus, develops a unified pretraining pipeline and standardized benchmark across 16 downstream datasets, and instantiates SurgRec-MAE and SurgRec-JEPA.
main_findings: SurgRec consistently outperforms self-supervised baselines and vision-language models across downstream datasets, while the abstract notes that VLMs are unreliable for fine-grained temporal recognition and sensitive to prompt phrasing.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract emphasizes limitations of prior surgical foundation models but does not specify concrete limitations of SurgRec itself.
future_opportunities: The released recipe, models, and benchmark are positioned as a foundation for building more general surgical video models.

title: JEPA-MSAC: A Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture for Multimodal Sensing-Assisted Communications
authors: Can Zheng; Jiguang He; Guofa Cai; Nannan Li; Mehdi Bennis; Henk Wymeersch; Merouane Debbah
principal_argument: A shared self-supervised multimodal predictive backbone can support several wireless PHY tasks with lower adaptation cost than task-specific supervised pipelines.
methodology: JEPA-MSAC maps multimodal sensing and communication measurements into a unified token space, pretrains a shared backbone with temporal block-masked JEPA, then freezes that backbone and trains lightweight heads for localization, beam prediction, and RSSI prediction.
main_findings: The learned latent state supports accurate multi-task prediction with low adaptation cost, and ablations highlight scaling behavior and the effect of key pretraining choices.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract does not provide headline numerical gains and focuses on a specific set of wireless prediction tasks.
future_opportunities: Extend the framework to more sensing modalities, communication settings, and downstream PHY tasks.

title: Semantic Interaction Information mediates compositional generalization in latent space
authors: John Schwarcz
principal_argument: Compositional generalization in latent-space tasks depends on modeling interactions among relevant latent variables, and Semantic Interaction Information captures how much those interactions matter for performance.
methodology: Frames compositional generalization as variational inference over interacting latent variables, introduces the Cognitive Gridworld POMDP and the Semantic Interaction Information metric, analyzes RNN variants, and proposes Representation Classification Chains with separate RL-based inference and self-supervised embedding modules.
main_findings: Semantic Interaction Information explains the accuracy gap between Echo State and fully trained networks, reveals a confidence-versus-accuracy failure mode, and the proposed RCC architecture generalizes to novel combinations of relevant variables.
limitations_or_gaps: The hard regime exposes a circular dependence between inferring latent variables and learning their interactions, and the evidence summarized in the abstract is centered on the Cognitive Gridworld setting.
future_opportunities: Use Cognitive Gridworld as a benchmark for continual meta-learning and goal-directed generalist agents, and extend RCC-style disentangling of inference and embeddings to richer environments.

title: Policy-Guided World Model Planning for Language-Conditioned Visual Navigation
authors: Amirhosein Chahe; Lifeng Zhou
principal_argument: Policy-guided initialization can make latent world-model planning more effective for language-conditioned visual navigation than either reactive policies or uninformed planning alone.
methodology: Builds PiJEPA as a two-stage system: an Octo-based navigation policy conditioned on frozen DINOv2 or V-JEPA-2 features is finetuned on CAST, then its action distribution warm-starts MPPI planning over a separately trained JEPA world model in the same latent space.
main_findings: Using the policy prior speeds planner convergence and improves goal-reaching and instruction-following, with PiJEPA outperforming standalone policy execution and uninformed world-model planning on real-world navigation tasks.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract focuses on CAST-based training and real-world navigation evaluation, while broader task coverage and system tradeoffs beyond this two-stage setup are not detailed.
future_opportunities: Test the policy-guided planning recipe on broader embodied tasks and continue exploring how encoder choice affects both policy and world-model components.

title: GazeQwen: Lightweight Gaze-Conditioned LLM Modulation for Streaming Video Understanding
authors: Trong Thang Pham; Hien Nguyen; Ngan Le
principal_argument: Learning gaze-conditioned hidden-state modulation is a more effective way to inject eye-gaze information into multimodal video models than prompt-style overlays or simply scaling model size.
methodology: Introduces GazeQwen, where a small gaze resampler combines V-JEPA 2.1 video features with fixation-based positional encodings and injects additive residuals into selected Qwen decoder layers, with an optional LoRA refinement stage, then evaluates on all StreamGaze tasks.
main_findings: GazeQwen reaches 63.9% accuracy on StreamGaze, outperforming the same backbone using gaze prompts by 16.1 points and GPT-4o by 10.5 points, the best result reported in the abstract.
limitations_or_gaps: The evidence in the abstract is concentrated on the StreamGaze benchmark, and it does not spell out deployment or latency tradeoffs beyond describing the approach as lightweight.
future_opportunities: Apply gaze-conditioned layer modulation to other streaming video or multimodal reasoning settings and study which injection locations are most useful.

title: A Wireless World Model for AI-Native 6G Networks
authors: Ziqi Chen; Yi Ren; Yixuan Huang; Qi Sun; Nan Li; Yuhong Huang; Chih-Lin I; Yifan Li; Liang Xia
principal_argument: Wireless channel modeling benefits from a physics-aware multimodal world model that internalizes causal links between 3D geometry and signal dynamics rather than relying on purely data-driven pattern matching.
methodology: Pretrains a multimodal mixture-of-experts Transformer world model on a large ray-traced dataset using a joint-embedding predictive architecture that fuses channel state information, 3D point clouds, and user trajectories, then validates on real measurements and five downstream tasks.
main_findings: The Wireless World Model performs strongly in seen environments, unseen generalization settings, and real-world measurements, consistently beating uni-modal foundation models and task-specific baselines across the supported downstream tasks.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract emphasizes large-scale multimodal pretraining and downstream wins, but does not detail failure modes or operational costs for deployment.
future_opportunities: Use the model as a foundation for physics-aware 6G intelligence and extend multimodal wireless world modeling to additional network tasks and environments.

title: Le MuMo JEPA: Multi-Modal Self-Supervised Representation Learning with Learnable Fusion Tokens
authors: Ciem Cornelissen; Sam Leroux; Pieter Simoens
principal_argument: Learnable fusion-token bottlenecks can make multimodal JEPA training efficient while preserving strong downstream performance across aligned sensor pairs.
methodology: Le MuMo JEPA extends LeJEPA with modality-specific patch stems, a shared transformer, learnable fusion tokens, pruned fusion after early cross-modal attention, and SIGReg on the joint multimodal CLS embedding; it is tested on RGB-depth and RGB-thermal settings.
main_findings: The model gives the strongest performance-efficiency trade-off among from-scratch multimodal baselines on Waymo, remains strongest under from-scratch training on nuScenes, and achieves the best FLIR results, especially after Waymo-initialized fine-tuning, while using less compute and memory.
limitations_or_gaps: The demonstrated scope is aligned RGB-depth and RGB-thermal perception benchmarks, so broader modality mixes are not covered in the summary.
future_opportunities: Apply the fusion-token design to additional sensor combinations and transfer settings beyond driving benchmarks.

title: Curriculum-Driven 3D CT Report Generation via Language-Free Visual Grafting and Zone-Constrained Compression
authors: V. K. Cody Bumgardner; Mitchell A. Klusty; Mahmut S. Gokmen; Evan W. Damron
principal_argument: Language-free self-supervised visual pretraining plus curriculum-based vision-language adaptation can improve 3D CT report generation while keeping the decoder grounded in patient-specific imaging content.
methodology: Presents Ker-VLJEPA-3B, a four-phase curriculum that adapts a Llama 3.2 3B decoder to frozen LeJEPA CT features, with zone-constrained cross-attention compression, PCA whitening, positive-findings-only training, warm bridge initialization, and selective freezing with elastic weight consolidation.
main_findings: On CT-RATE, the system reaches macro F1 0.429 versus a 0.414 prior state of the art, improves to 0.448 with threshold optimization, and ablations attribute 56.6% of generation quality to patient-specific visual content.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary is centered on thoracic CT and a positive-findings-only strategy, so broader report coverage and other imaging domains are not described in the abstract.
future_opportunities: Transfer the curriculum and language-free visual backbone design to other report-generation settings and other self-supervised medical encoders.

title: Pretext Matters: An Empirical Study of SSL Methods in Medical Imaging
authors: Vedrana Ivezić; Mara Pleasure; Ashwath Radhachandran; Saarang Panchavati; Shreeram Athreya; Vivek Sant; Benjamin Emert; Gregory Fishbein; Corey Arnold; William Speier
principal_argument: The best self-supervised objective in medical imaging depends on how clinically relevant signal is spatially organized within each modality.
methodology: Empirically compares joint embedding architectures and joint embedding predictive architectures on ultrasound and histopathology, then checks the clinical relevance of the learned features with board-certified radiologists and pathologists.
main_findings: JEAs work better when informative signal is spatially localized, as in histopathology, while JEPAs are better when useful information is globally structured, as in liver ultrasound.
limitations_or_gaps: The study summarized in the abstract covers two representative modalities, so the proposed matching framework still needs broader validation across medical imaging settings.
future_opportunities: Extend the modality-to-objective matching framework to additional imaging types, spatial structures, and noise regimes.

title: ThinkJEPA: Empowering Latent World Models with Large Vision-Language Reasoning Model
authors: Haichao Zhang; Yijiang Li; Shwai He; Tushar Nagarajan; Mingfei Chen; Jianglin Lu; Ang Li; Yun Fu
principal_argument: Long-horizon latent world modeling improves when dense JEPA dynamics are guided by semantically richer reasoning signals from a large vision-language model.
methodology: Combines a dense JEPA branch for fine-grained motion with a sparsely sampled VLM thinker branch for semantic guidance, using a hierarchical pyramid module to convert multi-layer VLM representations into features compatible with latent prediction.
main_findings: On hand-manipulation trajectory prediction, the method beats both a strong VLM-only baseline and a JEPA predictor baseline and produces more robust long-horizon rollouts.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract focuses on hand-manipulation trajectories and specifically motivates the method around VLM sparse sampling, language bottlenecks, and small-data adaptation issues.
future_opportunities: Apply VLM-guided JEPA world modeling to broader action-conditioned forecasting tasks and refine how reasoning signals are injected across time scales.

title: ARYA: A Physics-Constrained Composable & Deterministic World Model Architecture
authors: Seth Dobrin; Lukasz Chmiel
principal_argument: A deterministic, composable world model built from specialized nano models and protected by an immutable safety kernel can satisfy canonical world-model requirements without a monolithic neural foundation model.
methodology: Describes the ARYA architecture through five design principles, a hierarchical system of nano models orchestrated by the AARA agent, formal alignment to canonical world-model requirements, and high-level benchmark comparisons against named frontier systems.
main_findings: The abstract claims linear scaling, sparse activation, selective untraining, sub-20-second training cycles, an uncircumventable safety boundary, and state-of-the-art results on 6 of 9 benchmarks across seven industry nodes.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract is claim-dense and summarizes benchmark outcomes at a high level without detailing protocols, conditions, or the evidence behind each comparison.
future_opportunities: Independent validation of the reported benchmark claims and further study of composable safety-constrained world models across domain-specific deployments.

title: Var-JEPA: A Variational Formulation of the Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture -- Bridging Predictive and Generative Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Moritz Gögl; Christopher Yau
principal_argument: JEPA can be cast as a variational latent-variable model, and making that structure explicit yields a more principled predictive self-supervised objective with uncertainty estimates.
methodology: Var-JEPA derives a single-ELBO formulation from coupled latent-variable models, replacing heuristic anti-collapse regularization with an explicit variational objective; the paper instantiates this as Var-T-JEPA for tabular data.
main_findings: The framework produces meaningful representations without ad hoc anti-collapse regularizers, enables principled latent uncertainty quantification, and consistently improves over T-JEPA on tabular tasks while staying competitive with strong raw-feature baselines.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstracted evidence is for tabular data, so effectiveness on vision, audio, or video settings is not established here.
future_opportunities: Extend the variational JEPA formulation to other modalities and study uncertainty-aware downstream uses.

title: Structured Latent Dynamics in Wireless CSI via Homomorphic World Models
authors: Salmane Naoumi; Mehdi Bennis; Marwa Chafii
principal_argument: Wireless CSI can be modeled as structured latent dynamics, and imposing homomorphic geometric structure makes JEPA world models more predictive and spatially faithful.
methodology: Learns action-conditioned latent dynamics from CSI trajectories with a self-supervised JEPA framework and uses Lie-algebra-based homomorphic updates to structure transitions in latent space, then evaluates on DICHASUS.
main_findings: The method preserves topology and forecasts future embeddings better than strong baselines across unseen environments, while also producing metrically faithful channel charts.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract reports results on DICHASUS and latent-space quality, but does not detail how performance transfers to wider wireless deployments.
future_opportunities: Build downstream systems for mobility-aware scheduling, localization, and wireless scene understanding on top of the learned latent space.

title: Probing the Latent World: Emergent Discrete Symbols and Physical Structure in Latent Representations
authors: Liu hung ming
principal_argument: Frozen JEPA video encoders already contain structured physical information that can be exposed as discrete symbols through passive quantization without retraining the encoder.
methodology: Introduces the AI Mother Tongue probe, a lightweight vocabulary-free quantizer applied to frozen V-JEPA 2 latents, and evaluates symbol distributions on Kinetics-mini category-contrast experiments spanning grasp angle, object geometry, and temporal motion structure.
main_findings: Symbol distributions differ significantly across all three physical dimensions, and the latent space appears compact, with semantic differences expressed as graded distributional shifts rather than clean categorical partitions.
limitations_or_gaps: The paper is framed as Stage 1 of a larger roadmap, so it demonstrates passive probing of frozen representations rather than a full action-conditioned symbolic world model.
future_opportunities: Continue toward the proposed multi-stage action-conditioned symbolic world model and expand interpretability studies of frozen JEPA latent spaces.

title: LuMamba: Latent Unified Mamba for Electrode Topology-Invariant and Efficient EEG Modeling
authors: Danaé Broustail; Anna Tegon; Thorir Mar Ingolfsson; Yawei Li; Luca Benini
principal_argument: EEG foundation modeling can be made more topology-invariant and efficient by combining latent predictive pretraining with linear-complexity temporal modeling.
methodology: Builds LuMamba from LUNA-based channel unification, bidirectional Mamba temporal blocks, and LeJEPA-style self-supervision, pretraining on more than 21,000 hours of TUEG EEG and testing across five downstream tasks with different electrode layouts.
main_findings: Combining masked reconstruction with LeJEPA yields the strongest representations, enabling strong downstream accuracy, 377 times fewer FLOPS than state-of-the-art models at similar sequence lengths, and much longer sequence scaling.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract notes that LeJEPA alone gives diffuse embeddings and reconstruction alone is less generalizable, implying that the best results rely on a combined objective rather than a single pretext.
future_opportunities: Extend the approach to additional biosignals, electrode configurations, and pretraining-objective combinations.

title: Emergent Compositional Communication for Latent World Properties
authors: Tomek Kaszyński
principal_argument: Multi-agent communication pressure can extract discrete and compositional representations of hidden physical properties from frozen video features, and the backbone determines which properties are easiest to communicate.
methodology: Uses agents linked by a Gumbel-Softmax bottleneck with iterated learning, runs controls on bandwidth and temporal coverage, performs causal interventions, compares DINOv2 and V-JEPA 2, and validates on Physics 101 real-camera footage.
main_findings: With four agents, nearly all seeds converge to near-perfect compositionality; multi-agent structure, not simple bandwidth, drives the effect; DINOv2 is stronger on spatially visible ramp physics while V-JEPA 2 is stronger on dynamics-only collision physics; the learned protocol also supports planning and counterfactual reasoning.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract studies a defined set of latent physical properties and frozen backbones, and shows that performance depends strongly on communication structure and perceptual prior.
future_opportunities: Probe more physical properties, broaden real-world validation, and use the learned compositional protocol for richer planning and control.

title: Laya: A LeJEPA Approach to EEG via Latent Prediction over Reconstruction
authors: Saarang Panchavati; Uddhav Panchavati; Corey Arnold; William Speier
principal_argument: For EEG representation learning, latent prediction with LeJEPA is a more promising self-supervised objective than raw-signal reconstruction because reconstruction can overemphasize artifacts instead of neural structure.
methodology: Introduces Laya as the first EEG foundation model based on LeJEPA and compares it with reconstruction-based baselines across multiple EEG benchmarks, with emphasis on linear probing behavior.
main_findings: Laya improves linear-probe performance across the evaluated EEG benchmarks, supporting latent predictive objectives as a path to more transferable high-level EEG representations.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract itself notes that EEG foundation-model gains can be modest and sensitive to downstream adaptation and fine-tuning, and it summarizes results without detailing every benchmark condition.
future_opportunities: Further test LeJEPA-style EEG pretraining under varied downstream adaptation strategies and across broader clinical and BCI settings.

title: Auto Researching, not hyperparameter tuning: Convergence Analysis of 10,000 Experiments
authors: Xiaoyi Li
principal_argument: Autonomous LLM experimenters mainly succeed by discovering better architectural regions of the design space rather than by merely tuning hyperparameters.
methodology: Analyzes 10,469 LLM-designed experiments across 108,000 discrete configurations for dashcam collision detection over 27 days, using ANOVA, cross-task validation, convergence analysis, and multi-agent search-dynamics metrics.
main_findings: Architectural choices explain 94% of performance variance in the main study and 75% in the second dataset, the agents identify a V-JEPA 2 plus Zipformer combination that humans had not proposed, and guided search reaches strong performance faster than random search.
limitations_or_gaps: The evidence comes from collision-detection experiments plus one additional validation dataset, and the convergence analysis is explicitly post-bugfix.
future_opportunities: Apply the evaluation framework to other tasks and use the observed specialization and entropy dynamics to design stronger autonomous research agents.

title: Self-Distillation of Hidden Layers for Self-Supervised Representation Learning
authors: Scott C. Lowe; Anthony Fuller; Sageev Oore; Evan Shelhamer; Graham W. Taylor
principal_argument: Predicting multiple hidden-layer teacher representations can stabilize predictive self-supervised learning and capture richer abstractions than relying on reconstruction or final-layer targets alone.
methodology: Introduces Bootleg, a hierarchical self-distillation method that predicts latent targets from several teacher hidden layers, and evaluates it on image classification and semantic segmentation benchmarks against predictive and generative SSL baselines.
main_findings: Bootleg substantially outperforms comparable baselines, including about a 10% gain over I-JEPA on the classification results highlighted in the abstract, while also improving semantic segmentation performance.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract grounds the evidence in image classification and segmentation tasks and does not detail how the method behaves in other modalities or settings.
future_opportunities: Transfer hierarchical latent-target prediction to other modalities and other predictive SSL architectures.

title: V-JEPA 2.1: Unlocking Dense Features in Video Self-Supervised Learning
authors: Lorenzo Mur-Labadia; Matthew Muckley; Amir Bar; Mido Assran; Koustuv Sinha; Mike Rabbat; Yann LeCun; Nicolas Ballas; Adrien Bardes
principal_argument: Dense predictive objectives, hierarchical self-supervision, multimodal tokenization, and scale can substantially improve JEPA-style visual world modeling for images and videos.
methodology: V-JEPA 2.1 combines a dense masking-based predictive loss over visible and masked tokens, deep supervision across intermediate encoder layers, unified tokenizers for images and videos, and scaling in data and model size.
main_findings: The model achieves state-of-the-art benchmark results including 7.71 mAP on Ego4D short-term object-interaction anticipation, 40.8 Recall@5 on EPIC-KITCHENS high-level action anticipation, and a 20-point gain in real-robot grasping success over V-JEPA-2 AC, alongside strong navigation, depth, and recognition performance.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary emphasizes benchmark wins on the studied image, video, and robotics tasks without detailing failure modes or narrower deployment constraints.
future_opportunities: Scale the dense-feature recipe further and apply it to additional embodied, video-understanding, and robot-perception tasks.

title: Is the reconstruction loss culprit? An attempt to outperform JEPA
authors: Alexey Potapov; Oleg Shcherbakov; Ivan Kravchenko
principal_argument: JEPA may not owe its advantage purely to avoiding reconstruction loss; objective asymmetries and predictable-component selection also matter, and gated predictive autoencoders can surpass it on the studied toy system.
methodology: The paper compares JEPA-style predictive learning and reconstruction autoencoders on a controlled linear dynamical-system toy problem, performs diagnostic analyses with PCA baselines, and introduces gated predictive autoencoders that select predictable components.
main_findings: The apparent JEPA robustness advantage to noise weakens under closer diagnostics, and the proposed gated predictive autoencoder is stable across noise levels while matching or outperforming JEPA on the toy benchmark.
limitations_or_gaps: Results are explicitly confined to a toy linear dynamical system rather than real-world high-dimensional datasets.
future_opportunities: Test whether the gated predictive-autoencoder idea transfers to more realistic nonlinear representation-learning settings.

title: Sat-JEPA-Diff: Bridging Self-Supervised Learning and Generative Diffusion for Remote Sensing
authors: Kursat Komurcu; Linas Petkevicius
principal_argument: Combining JEPA-style semantic prediction with diffusion generation can better balance structural fidelity and texture realism in satellite-image forecasting.
methodology: Sat-JEPA-Diff uses an IJEPA module to predict stable semantic representations and conditions a frozen Stable Diffusion backbone through a lightweight cross-attention adapter, with evaluation on a global Sentinel-2 dataset.
main_findings: The method produces sharper boundaries, attains leading perceptual scores including GSSIM 0.8984 and FID 0.1475, and outperforms deterministic baselines despite standard autoregressive stability limits.
limitations_or_gaps: The reported evidence is tied to Sentinel-2 remote sensing and the summary still notes autoregressive stability limits.
future_opportunities: Assess the approach on more remote-sensing regimes and further improve long-horizon stability.

title: LeWorldModel: Stable End-to-End Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture from Pixels
authors: Lucas Maes; Quentin Le Lidec; Damien Scieur; Yann LeCun; Randall Balestriero
principal_argument: End-to-end JEPA world models can be trained stably from pixels with a very simple objective, avoiding the heavy stabilization machinery used by prior approaches.
methodology: LeWorldModel trains from raw pixels using only a next-embedding prediction loss and a Gaussian latent regularizer, with roughly 15M parameters on a single GPU, then evaluates control performance plus physical probing and surprise detection.
main_findings: The method is presented as the first JEPA to train stably end-to-end from raw pixels with only two loss terms, cuts tunable loss hyperparameters from six to one versus the only existing end-to-end alternative, plans up to 48x faster than foundation-model-based world models, and remains competitive across varied 2D and 3D control tasks.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract-level evidence is centered on control benchmarks and does not establish performance across broader world-modeling domains.
future_opportunities: Apply the simplified end-to-end JEPA recipe to additional environments and downstream planning or anomaly-detection settings.

title: Representation Learning for Spatiotemporal Physical Systems
authors: Helen Qu; Rudy Morel; Michael McCabe; Alberto Bietti; François Lanusse; Shirley Ho; Yann LeCun
principal_argument: Downstream scientific tasks are a better test of physically meaningful representations than next-frame emulation, and latent-space self-supervised methods can be more useful than pixel-level prediction objectives.
methodology: Compares general-purpose self-supervised methods and physics-oriented modeling approaches on downstream tasks for spatiotemporal physical systems, including governing-parameter estimation.
main_findings: Not all methods designed for physical modeling outperform generic self-supervised learning. Methods that learn in latent space, including JEPA-style approaches, outperform pixel-level prediction objectives on the evaluated downstream tasks.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary emphasizes downstream evaluation tasks such as parameter estimation and does not specify broader task coverage or detailed computational tradeoffs beyond the pitfalls of autoregressive emulators.
future_opportunities: Extend evaluation to more scientific inference tasks and further develop latent-space representation learning methods for physics-grounded modeling.

title: PVI: Plug-in Visual Injection for Vision-Language-Action Models
authors: Zezhou Zhang; Songxin Zhang; Xiao Xiong; Junjie Zhang; Zejian Xie; Jingyi Xi; Zunyao Mao; Zan Mao; Zhixin Mai; Zhuoyang Song; Jiaxing Zhang
principal_argument: A lightweight plug-in module can inject auxiliary visual cues into vision-language-action models without major architectural changes, and temporal video features are especially valuable for action prediction.
methodology: Introduces Plug-in Visual Injection (PVI), an encoder-agnostic module with zero-initialized residual pathways attached to a pretrained action expert, then compares temporal video features such as V-JEPA2 against static features such as DINOv2 in simulation and real-robot settings.
main_findings: PVI delivers consistent gains over the base policy and other injection strategies. Temporal video features from V-JEPA2 outperform strong static image features, with the biggest gains on multi-phase tasks; real-robot cloth-folding results support practical usefulness.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary highlights specific simulated tasks and a long-horizon cloth-folding demonstration, but does not establish how broadly the method transfers across robot platforms or task families.
future_opportunities: Explore broader long-horizon manipulation settings and additional temporal feature sources within lightweight plug-in architectures.

title: Zero-Shot Cross-City Generalization in End-to-End Autonomous Driving: Self-Supervised versus Supervised Representations
authors: Fatemeh Naeinian; Ali Hamza; Haoran Zhu; Anna Choromanska
principal_argument: Zero-shot transfer to unseen cities exposes major failure modes in end-to-end driving, and self-supervised visual representations improve robustness relative to supervised backbones.
methodology: Integrates self-supervised backbones including I-JEPA, DINOv2, and MAE into planning frameworks, then evaluates strict geographic train-test splits on nuScenes in open-loop and NAVSIM in closed-loop.
main_findings: Models with traditional supervised backbones show a large cross-city generalization gap, especially when transferring across different driving conventions. Self-supervised pretraining sharply reduces this gap and improves closed-loop PDMS by up to 4 percent.
limitations_or_gaps: The reported evidence is tied to the listed backbones, datasets, and evaluation protocols, and the summary does not claim coverage of other driving benchmarks or planning setups.
future_opportunities: Make zero-shot geographic transfer a standard evaluation and investigate domain-specific self-supervised pretraining for more robust planning.

title: Dreamer-CDP: Improving Reconstruction-free World Models Via Continuous Deterministic Representation Prediction
authors: Michael Hauri; Friedemann Zenke
principal_argument: Reconstruction-free world models can remain competitive if they predict continuous deterministic representations with a JEPA-style objective instead of reconstructing pixels.
methodology: Introduces a JEPA-style predictor over continuous deterministic latent representations within a Dreamer-style model-based reinforcement learning setup and evaluates it on Crafter.
main_findings: The method matches Dreamer on Crafter, showing that effective world model learning is possible on this benchmark without reconstruction objectives.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary reports results on Crafter only and does not describe performance on additional environments or tasks.
future_opportunities: Test JEPA-style reconstruction-free world models on a wider range of high-dimensional reinforcement-learning benchmarks.

title: Weak-SIGReg: Covariance Regularization for Stable Deep Learning
authors: Habibullah Akbar
principal_argument: SIGReg, introduced in LeJEPA, can serve as a general optimization stabilizer for supervised deep learning, and a covariance-focused weak variant preserves the benefit with lower cost.
methodology: Derives Weak-SIGReg as a random-sketching approximation that targets covariance structure, motivates it through a representation-collapse perspective, and evaluates it on ViTs and deep vanilla MLPs.
main_findings: Weak-SIGReg recovers a collapsed ViT on CIFAR-100 from 20.73% to 72.02% accuracy and substantially improves convergence for deep vanilla MLPs trained with pure SGD.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary centers on selected supervised settings and does not specify how uniformly the method works across broader architectures, datasets, or optimization regimes.
future_opportunities: Apply Gaussian-constrained regularization more broadly as an architecture-agnostic training stabilizer and study its behavior in additional low-data or collapse-prone regimes.

title: Improving Diffusion Planners by Self-Supervised Action Gating with Energies
authors: Yuan Lu; Dongqi Han; Yansen Wang; Dongsheng Li
principal_argument: Diffusion planners need a feasibility signal beyond value estimates, and JEPA-based latent prediction error can provide an effective inference-time action gate.
methodology: Trains a JEPA encoder on offline state sequences together with an action-conditioned latent predictor, then re-ranks sampled trajectories by combining value scores with an energy based on latent prediction error.
main_findings: SAGE improves the performance and robustness of diffusion planners across locomotion, navigation, and manipulation benchmarks without environment rollouts or policy retraining.
limitations_or_gaps: The method is described for diffusion planning pipelines that already sample candidate trajectories and score them with values; the summary does not address other planner classes.
future_opportunities: Extend latent feasibility re-ranking to other offline planning pipelines and study its effects on harder long-horizon decision problems.

title: Social-JEPA: Emergent Geometric Isomorphism
authors: Haoran Zhang; Youjin Wang; Yi Duan; Rong Fu; Dianyu Zhao; Sicheng Fan; Shuaishuai Cao; Wentao Guo; Xiao Zhou
principal_argument: Independent agents can learn latent spaces that become approximately isometric when trained predictively on the same world, enabling direct cross-agent transfer.
methodology: Separate agents learn world models from different viewpoints without coordination or parameter sharing; the paper then analyzes latent-space geometry, zero-update classifier transfer, and distillation-like migration between agents.
main_findings: The learned latent spaces exhibit an approximate linear isometry despite large viewpoint shifts and limited pixel overlap, allowing classifier transfer without extra gradient steps and reducing later learning compute through migration.
limitations_or_gaps: The setup focuses on agents observing the same environment from different viewpoints, so broader interoperability claims remain limited at the abstract level.
future_opportunities: Probe whether the emergent alignment persists across more heterogeneous agents, tasks, and environments.

title: GeoWorld: Geometric World Models
authors: Zeyu Zhang; Danning Li; Ian Reid; Richard Hartley
principal_argument: Geometric structure matters in predictive world models, and hyperbolic JEPA representations can better preserve hierarchy and support longer-horizon visual planning.
methodology: Introduces GeoWorld with a Hyperbolic JEPA that maps Euclidean latents onto hyperbolic manifolds, plus geometric reinforcement learning for energy-based optimization, and evaluates on CrossTask and COIN.
main_findings: GeoWorld improves success rate by about 3% in 3-step planning and 2% in 4-step planning relative to the state-of-the-art V-JEPA 2 baseline.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary reports gains on CrossTask and COIN with short multi-step planning settings, and does not establish broader generality beyond those benchmarks.
future_opportunities: Explore geometric latent spaces for longer-horizon planning and additional energy-based world-model applications.

title: Semantic Tube Prediction: Beating LLM Data Efficiency with JEPA
authors: Hai Huang; Yann LeCun; Randall Balestriero
principal_argument: A JEPA-style geometric regularizer for language hidden states can dramatically improve LLM data efficiency beyond standard scaling-law expectations.
methodology: The paper introduces the Geodesic Hypothesis and Semantic Tube Prediction, which constrains hidden-state trajectories to a tubular neighborhood around a semantic geodesic, serving as a JEPA-style regularizer without explicit multi-view augmentations.
main_findings: Semantic Tube Prediction improves signal-to-noise ratio, preserves diversity by preventing trajectory collisions during inference, and matches baseline accuracy with 16x less training data on NL-RX-SYNTH.
limitations_or_gaps: The reported evidence is on NL-RX-SYNTH, so broader language benchmarks and model scales are not covered in the summary.
future_opportunities: Test the geometric regularizer on wider LLM workloads and study how semantic-manifold priors interact with scaling laws.

title: Knowledge, Rules and Their Embeddings: Two Paths towards Neuro-Symbolic JEPA
authors: Yongchao Huang; Hassan Raza
principal_argument: JEPA can bridge statistical learning and symbolic reasoning by both injecting rule structure into predictive representations and relaxing rules into continuous spaces for generation and discovery.
methodology: The proposed RiJEPA framework adds Energy-Based Constraints and a multimodal dual-encoder to encode logical biases, then uses differentiable rule relaxations with gradient-guided Langevin diffusion for continuous rule discovery, conditional generation, and inference.
main_findings: Empirical evaluations on synthetic topological simulations and a clinical use case are reported to confirm the framework's efficacy and support robust, generative, and interpretable neuro-symbolic representation learning.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary does not report quantitative results and only names synthetic and one clinical use case as evidence.
future_opportunities: Validate the framework on broader neuro-symbolic domains and further study scalable rule discovery and interpretability.

title: US-JEPA: A Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture for Medical Ultrasound
authors: Ashwath Radhachandran; Vedrana Ivezić; Shreeram Athreya; Ronit Anilkumar; Corey W. Arnold; William Speier
principal_argument: For ultrasound, predicting masked latent targets from a frozen domain-specific teacher offers a more stable and efficient self-supervised route than reconstruction or EMA-teacher JEPA variants.
methodology: US-JEPA applies the SALT objective with a frozen domain-specific teacher, decoupling student and teacher optimization, and benchmarks representation quality through linear probing across multiple UltraBench classification tasks against available ultrasound foundation models.
main_findings: US-JEPA achieves competitive or superior linear-probe results relative to domain-specific and universal vision foundation model baselines, supporting masked latent prediction as a stable path for ultrasound representation learning.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract centers on UltraBench linear-probe classification, so broader downstream clinical tasks are not detailed.
future_opportunities: Extend the frozen-teacher JEPA approach to more ultrasound tasks and other medical-imaging settings.

title: JEPA-DNA: Grounding Genomic Foundation Models through Joint-Embedding Predictive Architectures
authors: Ariel Larey; Elay Dahan; Amit Bleiweiss; Raizy Kellerman; Guy Leib; Omri Nayshool; Dan Ofer; Tal Zinger; Dan Dominissini; Gideon Rechavi; Nicole Bussola; Simon Lee; Shane O'Connell; Dung Hoang; Marissa Wirth; Alexander W. Charney; Nati Daniel; Yoli Shavit
principal_argument: Adding JEPA-style latent grounding to genomic pretraining can capture higher-level functional context that MLM or next-token prediction alone tends to miss.
methodology: JEPA-DNA combines standard generative objectives with latent-space supervision on a CLS token so the model predicts functional embeddings of masked genomic segments; it can be used from scratch or as continual pretraining for existing genomic foundation models.
main_findings: Across diverse genomic benchmarks, JEPA-DNA consistently outperforms generative-only baselines on supervised and zero-shot tasks and yields more biologically grounded representations.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary does not break out benchmark-specific results and is specific to genomic sequence modeling.
future_opportunities: Apply latent grounding to additional biological-sequence models and more genomic foundation-model pipelines.

title: GOT-JEPA: Generic Object Tracking with Model Adaptation and Occlusion Handling using Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture
authors: Shih-Fang Chen; Jun-Cheng Chen; I-Hong Jhuo; Yen-Yu Lin
principal_argument: Predicting tracking models rather than only image features can improve generic object tracking generalization and fine-grained occlusion handling.
methodology: GOT-JEPA uses a teacher predictor to generate pseudo-tracking models from a clean current frame and trains a student to match them from a corrupted frame given the same history; it is paired with OccuSolver for object-aware visibility estimation and iterative occlusion refinement.
main_findings: The framework provides stable pseudo supervision under occlusions, distractors, and adverse observations, while OccuSolver improves visibility labels and occlusion handling; evaluations on seven benchmarks indicate stronger generalization and robustness.
limitations_or_gaps: The abstract does not provide headline numerical improvements and focuses specifically on generic object tracking and occlusion reasoning.
future_opportunities: Extend predictive tracking-model pretraining and iterative occlusion reasoning to broader tracking conditions and scene dynamics.

title: MeFEm: Medical Face Embedding model
authors: Yury Borets; Stepan Botman
principal_argument: A domain-adapted JEPA can produce strong facial embeddings for biometric and medical analysis even with less training data than major baselines.
methodology: Builds MeFEm by modifying JEPA with axial stripe masking, circular loss weighting, and probabilistic CLS-token reassignment, then evaluates it on anthropometric tasks and BMI estimation from facial images.
main_findings: MeFEm outperforms FaRL and Franca on core anthropometric tasks while using substantially less data, and it shows promising BMI-estimation results on a newly consolidated dataset.
limitations_or_gaps: The BMI evaluation relies on a closed-source dataset, and the summary focuses on selected anthropometric and BMI tasks rather than broader clinical deployment.
future_opportunities: Use the released model as a baseline for broader facial medical-analysis work and develop better datasets that reduce domain bias.

title: Benchmarking Video Foundation Models for Remote Parkinson's Disease Screening
authors: Md Saiful Islam; Ekram Hossain; Abdelrahman Abdelkader; Tariq Adnan; Fazla Rabbi Mashrur; Sooyong Park; Praveen Kumar; Qasim Sudais; Natalia Chunga; Nami Shah; Jan Freyberg; Christopher Kanan; Ruth Schneider; Ehsan Hoque
principal_argument: Video foundation models can support remote Parkinson's screening, but model effectiveness depends strongly on the clinical task, so architecture choice should be task-aware.
methodology: Benchmarks seven state-of-the-art video foundation models on a new dataset of 1,888 participants across 16 standardized clinical tasks using frozen embeddings and a linear classification head.
main_findings: VideoPrism is strongest for visual speech and facial expressivity, V-JEPA is best for upper-limb motor tasks, and TimeSformer remains competitive for rhythmic tasks. Results reach AUCs of 76.4-85.3% and accuracies of 71.5-80.6%, with high specificity but lower sensitivity.
limitations_or_gaps: Lower sensitivity limits standalone screening performance, and the summary explicitly points to the need for calibration and combining multiple tasks or modalities.
future_opportunities: Develop task-aware model selection and integrate multiple tasks or modalities for stronger remote neurological screening systems.

title: Self-Supervised JEPA-based World Models for LiDAR Occupancy Completion and Forecasting
authors: Haoran Zhu; Anna Choromanska
principal_argument: A self-supervised JEPA world model can learn useful LiDAR representations for modeling spatiotemporal scene evolution in autonomous driving.
methodology: Introduces AD-LiST-JEPA, a JEPA-based world model trained on unlabeled LiDAR data, and evaluates the learned encoder on LiDAR occupancy completion and forecasting.
main_findings: Proof-of-concept experiments show that JEPA pretraining improves downstream occupancy completion and forecasting performance.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary reports proof-of-concept results centered on the OCF downstream task; broader long-horizon planning validation is not described.
future_opportunities: Scale self-supervised LiDAR world-model pretraining and test whether the learned representations improve longer-horizon planning and driving tasks.

title: Intrinsic-Energy Joint Embedding Predictive Architectures Induce Quasimetric Spaces
authors: Anthony Kobanda; Waris Radji
principal_argument: Intrinsic JEPA energy functions form quasimetrics, linking JEPA representation learning with quasimetric reinforcement learning and asymmetric goal-reaching dynamics.
methodology: Presents a theoretical analysis of intrinsic least-action JEPA energies under closure and additivity assumptions, and compares them with goal-reaching cost-to-go functions.
main_findings: Shows that any intrinsic energy is a quasimetric, that optimal cost-to-go functions admit this intrinsic form, and that symmetric finite energies mismatch one-way reachability.
limitations_or_gaps: This is a short theoretical article; the summary does not describe empirical validation beyond the conceptual connection.
future_opportunities: Use asymmetric intrinsic-energy JEPA objectives in goal-conditioned control and reinforcement-learning settings where directionality matters.

title: JEPA-VLA: Video Predictive Embedding is Needed for VLA Models
authors: Shangchen Miao; Ningya Feng; Jialong Wu; Ye Lin; Xu He; Dong Li; Mingsheng Long
principal_argument: Video-predictive embeddings, especially V-JEPA 2, address key weaknesses in existing visual representations for vision-language-action models.
methodology: Analyzes shortcomings of current VLA visual representations, then introduces JEPA-VLA to adaptively integrate predictive video embeddings into existing VLAs and evaluates it on simulation and real-robot benchmarks.
main_findings: Predictive embeddings better capture task-relevant temporal dynamics and JEPA-VLA delivers substantial gains on LIBERO, LIBERO-plus, RoboTwin2.0, and real-robot tasks.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary emphasizes benchmark gains but does not detail compute trade-offs, failure modes, or settings where predictive embeddings may be less helpful.
future_opportunities: Integrate video-predictive pretraining more broadly into robotic policy learning to improve sample efficiency and generalization.

title: Causal-JEPA: Learning World Models through Object-Level Latent Interventions
authors: Heejeong Nam; Quentin Le Lidec; Lucas Maes; Yann LeCun; Randall Balestriero
principal_argument: Object-level masking in JEPA-style world models induces a causal inductive bias that improves interaction reasoning and planning efficiency.
methodology: Proposes C-JEPA, which extends masked joint embedding prediction from patches to object-centric representations using object-level masking, and evaluates it on visual question answering and control tasks alongside a formal analysis.
main_findings: C-JEPA improves counterfactual reasoning by about 20% absolutely, reaches comparable control with only 1% of the latent input features of patch-based models, and is supported by a formal causal analysis.
limitations_or_gaps: The approach depends on object-centric representations, and the summary only reports results on the listed VQA and control settings.
future_opportunities: Apply object-level latent interventions to broader world-model, reasoning, and control problems where interactions matter.

title: Predictive Associative Memory: Retrieval Beyond Similarity Through Temporal Co-occurrence
authors: Jason Dury
principal_argument: Associative memory should retrieve states linked by temporal co-occurrence, not just representational similarity, and JEPA-style prediction can learn that associative structure.
methodology: Proposes Predictive Associative Memory with an Inward JEPA over stored experience paired with an Outward JEPA over incoming data, then evaluates associative recall on a synthetic benchmark against cosine similarity.
main_findings: The model retrieves true temporal associates with high precision, recovers cross-boundary associations that cosine similarity misses, separates co-experienced from never-co-experienced states well, and remains stable across seeds. A temporal shuffle control supports that the signal comes from temporal co-occurrence.
limitations_or_gaps: The evaluation is on a synthetic benchmark and is framed around recall of experienced associations rather than generalization to unseen ones.
future_opportunities: Apply temporally grounded associative retrieval to richer experience streams and more practical memory systems.

title: VLA-JEPA: Enhancing Vision-Language-Action Model with Latent World Model
authors: Jingwen Sun; Wenyao Zhang; Zekun Qi; Shaojie Ren; Zezhi Liu; Hanxin Zhu; Guangzhong Sun; Xin Jin; Zhibo Chen
principal_argument: Leakage-free latent prediction lets VLA pretraining focus on action-relevant state transitions rather than pixel variation or nuisance motion.
methodology: Introduces VLA-JEPA, where a target encoder provides latent targets from future frames while the student sees only the current observation, followed by action-head fine-tuning.
main_findings: Across LIBERO, LIBERO-Plus, SimplerEnv, and real-world manipulation tasks, VLA-JEPA improves robustness and generalization over prior methods.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary highlights benchmark gains but does not describe detailed ablations, limitations, or behavior outside the reported manipulation settings.
future_opportunities: Extend leakage-free JEPA pretraining to broader robotic policies and internet-scale video learning pipelines.

title: Online Monitoring Framework for Automotive Time Series Data using JEPA Embeddings
authors: Alexander Fertig; Karthikeyan Chandra Sekaran; Lakshman Balasubramanian; Michael Botsch
principal_argument: JEPA-based self-supervised embeddings can support online anomaly monitoring for automotive time-series data without anomaly labels.
methodology: Builds a JEPA-based self-supervised prediction task to learn object-state embeddings, then applies established anomaly-detection methods to those embeddings and evaluates on nuScenes.
main_findings: The learned JEPA embeddings provide expressive latent representations that enable anomaly identification in real-world automotive data.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary focuses on object-state monitoring and nuScenes-based experiments, without describing broader deployment results or labeled anomaly comparisons.
future_opportunities: Deploy the framework in live vehicle monitoring pipelines and test it on a wider range of unknown anomaly types and operational conditions.

title: BiJEPA: Bi-directional Joint Embedding Predictive Architecture for Symmetric Representation Learning
authors: Yongchao Huang
principal_argument: Bi-directional JEPA with cycle consistency can learn more symmetric representations than standard uni-directional JEPA when stabilized appropriately.
methodology: Proposes BiJEPA with bi-directional prediction and a critical norm regularization mechanism, then evaluates it on synthetic periodic signals, Lorenz attractors, and MNIST.
main_findings: BiJEPA converges stably without collapse, captures semantic structure in chaotic systems, and learns robust representations that support generation and generalisation.
limitations_or_gaps: The reported evaluation is limited to synthetic and benchmark datasets in the summary, rather than larger real-world applications.
future_opportunities: Test bidirectional predictive objectives on more complex modalities and larger-scale representation-learning problems.

title: SurgMotion: A Video-Native Foundation Model for Universal Understanding of Surgical Videos
authors: Jinlin Wu; Felix Holm; Chuxi Chen; An Wang; Yaxin Hu; Xiaofan Ye; Zelin Zang; Miao Xu; Lihua Zhou; Huai Liao; Danny T. M. Chan; Ming Feng; Wai S. Poon; Hongliang Ren; Dong Yi; Nassir Navab; Gaofeng Meng; Jiebo Luo; Hongbin Liu; Zhen Lei
principal_argument: For surgical videos, latent motion prediction is a better pretraining signal than pixel reconstruction because it prioritizes semantic dynamics over low-level visual artifacts.
methodology: Builds SurgMotion on V-JEPA with motion-guided latent masked prediction, spatiotemporal affinity self-distillation, and feature-diversity regularization, then pretrains on the SurgMotion-15M dataset and evaluates across 17 benchmarks.
main_findings: SurgMotion substantially outperforms prior methods across surgical workflow recognition, action triplet recognition, skill assessment, polyp segmentation, and depth estimation, positioning it as a strong universal surgical-video model.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary emphasizes benchmark gains and large-scale pretraining, but does not specify deployment constraints or behavior beyond the listed surgical tasks.
future_opportunities: Extend motion-oriented surgical foundation modeling to additional procedures, tasks, and large-scale clinical video resources.

title: MTS-JEPA: Multi-Resolution Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture for Time-Series Anomaly Prediction
authors: Yanan He; Yunshi Wen; Xin Wang; Tengfei Ma
principal_argument: A multi-resolution JEPA with a soft codebook bottleneck can capture anomaly precursors across time scales while avoiding collapse.
methodology: Introduces MTS-JEPA, combining a multi-resolution predictive objective with a soft codebook bottleneck that separates transient shocks, long-term trends, and regime transitions.
main_findings: The proposed design prevents degenerate solutions and achieves state-of-the-art results under the early-warning protocol on standard benchmarks.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary reports benchmark performance but does not discuss deployment constraints or behavior beyond the evaluated early-warning setting.
future_opportunities: Apply multi-resolution JEPA anomaly prediction to critical infrastructure monitoring and other multivariate time-series domains.

title: A Lightweight Library for Energy-Based Joint-Embedding Predictive Architectures
authors: Basile Terver; Randall Balestriero; Megi Dervishi; David Fan; Quentin Garrido; Tushar Nagarajan; Koustuv Sinha; Wancong Zhang; Mike Rabbat; Yann LeCun; Amir Bar
principal_argument: A lightweight, modular library can make energy-based JEPA methods accessible across image, video, and action-conditioned world-model settings.
methodology: Presents the EB-JEPA open-source library with single-GPU examples, plus ablations on CIFAR-10, a Moving MNIST video example, and an action-conditioned Two Rooms world-model task.
main_findings: The library demonstrates 91% probing accuracy on CIFAR-10, scalable multi-step video prediction, 97% planning success on Two Rooms, and the importance of regularization for avoiding collapse.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary emphasizes lightweight examples and educational accessibility rather than broad production-scale benchmarks.
future_opportunities: Use the library as a base for research, teaching, and more extensive experiments on JEPA representation learning and world models.

title: Cell-JEPA: Latent Representation Learning for Single-Cell Transcriptomics
authors: Ali ElSheikh; Rui-Xi Wang; Weimin Wu; Yibo Wen; Payam Dibaeinia; Jennifer Yuntong Zhang; Jerry Yao-Chieh Hu; Mei Knudson; Sudarshan Babu; Shao-Hua Sun; Aly A. Khan; Han Liu
principal_argument: Predicting latent cell embeddings from partial observations can learn dropout-robust single-cell representations better than masked count reconstruction.
methodology: Introduces Cell-JEPA, which predicts cell-level embeddings from partial gene observations, and evaluates zero-shot cell-type clustering and perturbation prediction.
main_findings: Cell-JEPA improves zero-shot transfer clustering over scGPT and improves absolute-state reconstruction in perturbation prediction, though not effect-size estimation.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary explicitly notes that effect-size estimation does not improve, indicating incomplete gains for perturbation modeling.
future_opportunities: Combine JEPA-based representation learning with stronger perturbation-modeling methods for more complete cellular prediction.

title: CryoLVM: Self-supervised Learning from Cryo-EM Density Maps with Large Vision Models
authors: Weining Fu; Kai Shu; Kui Xu; Qiangfeng Cliff Zhang
principal_argument: A JEPA-based cryo-EM foundation model can learn transferable structural representations that support multiple downstream restoration and enhancement tasks.
methodology: Introduces CryoLVM by combining JEPA with a SCUNet-based backbone and a histogram-based distribution-alignment loss, then adapts the model to sharpening, super-resolution, and missing-wedge restoration.
main_findings: CryoLVM consistently outperforms strong baselines across multiple density-map quality metrics on all three evaluated cryo-EM tasks.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary focuses on density maps with resolved structures and on three downstream tasks, without claiming broader coverage of other cryo-EM settings.
future_opportunities: Use unified self-supervised structural pretraining for a wider range of cryo-EM analysis tasks.

title: Rectified LpJEPA: Joint-Embedding Predictive Architectures with Sparse and Maximum-Entropy Representations
authors: Yilun Kuang; Yash Dagade; Tim G. J. Rudner; Randall Balestriero; Yann LeCun
principal_argument: JEPA regularization should support sparse, non-negative, maximum-entropy representations rather than only dense Gaussian-like ones.
methodology: Introduces Rectified Distribution Matching Regularization to align representations with a Rectified Generalized Gaussian distribution, yielding Rectified LpJEPA.
main_findings: Rectified LpJEPA learns sparse, non-negative representations with strong sparsity-performance trade-offs and competitive image-classification performance.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary focuses on image-classification benchmarks, so broader modality coverage or downstream impacts are not described.
future_opportunities: Explore sparsity-controlled JEPA objectives in additional tasks and modalities where efficient representations may matter.

title: Gaussian-Constrained LeJEPA Representations for Unsupervised Scene Discovery and Pose Consistency
authors: Mohsen Mostafa
principal_argument: Gaussian-constrained embeddings inspired by LeJEPA can improve unsupervised scene discovery and pose consistency in ambiguous multi-scene image collections.
methodology: Evaluates three increasingly refined pipelines on the IMC2025 challenge, culminating in a LeJEPA-inspired approach that enforces isotropic Gaussian constraints on image embeddings for clustering and pose estimation.
main_findings: Gaussian-constrained embeddings improve scene separation and pose plausibility over heuristic baselines, especially in visually ambiguous settings.
limitations_or_gaps: The work is explicitly empirical rather than theoretical, and the summary grounds the evidence in the IMC2025 setting.
future_opportunities: Further connect self-supervised representation constraints with practical structure-from-motion pipelines and test them under broader real-world ambiguity.

title: Soft Clustering Anchors for Self-Supervised Speech Representation Learning in Joint Embedding Prediction Architectures
authors: Georgios Ioannides; Adrian Kieback; Judah Goldfeder; Linsey Pang; Aman Chadha; Aaron Elkins; Yann LeCun; Ravid Shwartz-Ziv
principal_argument: Speech JEPAs benefit from explicit grounding, and one-shot soft clustering anchors from a frozen GMM can stabilize training without iterative reclustering.
methodology: Fits a Gaussian Mixture Model once on log-mel spectrograms and uses its frozen soft posteriors as auxiliary targets with a decaying supervision schedule during JEPA training on roughly 50k hours of speech.
main_findings: GMM anchoring improves ASR, emotion recognition, and slot filling relative to a matched-compute WavLM-style baseline, while producing far more uniform cluster utilization.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary compares primarily against a WavLM-style baseline and does not detail broader compute tradeoffs or comparisons to more methods.
future_opportunities: Scale soft-anchored speech representation learning further and investigate other non-iterative grounding signals for JEPA training.

title: The Patient is not a Moving Document: A World Model Training Paradigm for Longitudinal EHR
authors: Irsyad Adam; Zekai Chen; David Laprade; Shaun Porwal; David Laub; Erik Reinertsen; Arda Pekis; Kevin Brown
principal_argument: Clinical foundation models should model patients as evolving dynamical systems rather than static documents, and combining JEPA with next-token prediction better captures disease trajectories.
methodology: Introduces SMB-Structure, a structured-EHR world model that pairs next-token prediction with latent JEPA prediction, and evaluates learned embeddings with linear probes across disease trajectories in two large cohorts.
main_findings: The training paradigm learns embeddings that capture disease dynamics not recovered by autoregressive baselines and achieves competitive performance on complex, heterogeneous clinical tasks.
limitations_or_gaps: The summary is centered on structured EHR data and two cohorts, and it does not specify broader deployment evidence beyond those settings.
